abilities.name: Magical Damage Block
abilities.description: Consumes a charge to block a fixed portion magical damage from damage instances over a certain value.
details: Infused Raindrops has the highest priority in the group of damage negating abilities, blocking damage before any of them do. When combined with other damage-negating effects (e.g. Aphotic Shield or Refraction ), Infused Raindrops negate the damage while the others do not. Has a higher priority than damage return effects (e.g. Dispersion and Blade Mail ), meaning it first blocks damage before it. Has a lower priority than Mana Shield , meaning Mana Shield first reduces damage before Infused Raindrop blocks it.
Only triggers on magical damage equal or greater than 75 ( before reductions ) coming from abilities and attacks, excluding self-inflicted damage and damage dealt by allies.
Ignores every form of incoming damage amplification , but does not ignore outgoing damage amplification .

Does not stack with other magical damage barriers . If multiple sources of barriers are active on a unit, all of them lose capacity from the same absorbed damage instance. However, if a damage instance is greater than the capacity of all barriers individually, the damage gets blocked as if the barriers were one barrier with combined capacity .
Neither consumes charges nor blocks any damage while on cooldown.

details: Town Portal Scroll refunding mechanics: The refunding triggers when Boots of Travel enters the hero's main inventory for the first time. Does not trigger when in the backpack , the stash , or in the Courier 's inventory. Triggers a second time upon upgrading the Boots of Travel, following the same rules as before. The refunding is bound to the item, not the hero. Acquiring Boots of Travel a second time triggers it again. Disassembling and Reassembling Boots of Travel triggers it again as well, since it is considered a new item. The refunding mechanic only considers Town Portal Scrolls within the dedicated slot. Scrolls within the stash or on the ground are ignored and not refunded. The refund value is based on the current sellback value: (100 if within the grace period, 50 otherwise).
